GracePrinciples.Principle: GRACE 4 current project state lives under .grace and future agents should treat .grace as durable project context.
Contract-first changes: clarify purpose, scope, inputs, outputs, acceptance, and verification before implementation unless the user asks for a bounded direct fix.
Use the smallest correct change that satisfies the request and preserve repository terminology.
Keep semantic anchors stable and grep-friendly. File-local anchors include START_MODULE_CONTRACT, START_MODULE_MAP, START_CONTRACT, START_BLOCK, and START_CHANGE_SUMMARY.
Semantic GRACE anchors are XML tags, never attributes. Module IDs use M-*, data flows use DF-*, graph documents use GD-*, verification entries use V-M-*, and change bundles use C-*.
src is the source of truth for code behavior; dist is generated output and is never edited manually.
Config safety matters: prefer conservative, idempotent writes and never silently clobber user-owned config.
Verification is a first-class artifact. Completion claims require fresh evidence from deterministic tests, typecheck, lint, build, pack, release, or GRACE checks as applicable.
Workflow automation uses explicit work-item IDs and simple counters, not semantic clustering or fuzzy similarity.
NEEDS_CONTEXT and BLOCKED statuses halt automation and return control to the main session or user.
Repository memory and historical docs are advisory below explicit user instructions, code, tests, and current .grace state.
Do not create retroactive C-* change bundles for completed historical work.
